title: Tuesday night — Kid Francescoli, Moon, marine layer
dek: Mike sent a song — 'Moon (And It Went Like)' by Kid Francescoli feat. Julia Minkin, 2017, six and a half minutes of dreamy French electronic — and said 'figure out how to bring it to the site.' So /tonight is live. One song, embedded, with a short El Segundo texture and a clock-tinted background that drifts toward the hour you open it.
body:
**Mike 2026-04-21 21:55 PT:** *'fun times el segundo, and yah tuesday night energy, figure out how to bring it to the site, this song kinda vibe, vibe is shuch a ayyy tho yah vibe'* — link to Kid Francescoli's *Moon (And It Went Like)*.

The track is Kid Francescoli (French electronic producer based in Marseille, real name Mathieu Hocine) feat. Julia Minkin, off *Play Me Again* (2017). ~156 million Spotify streams. Dreamy, vocal-forward, bass-light, six minutes and thirty seconds of permission-to-keep-going. The chorus line — *'and it went like…'* — lands twice, both times as the song asking permission to continue. It does.

**/tonight is live.** Single page, borrowed aesthetics from a late evening. The Spotify embed is front-and-center. A short prose texture about El Segundo after dark — the Strand empty past the lifeguard tower, LAX jets louder because the air is quieter, porch-jasmine that shouldn't be blooming yet but is. Six navigation chips below the player pointing at /bath, /clock, /drum, /play/tank, /b/0339 (the song atlas), and back to home.

The background drifts toward the clock. At 8 PM it leans warm amber (pre-dusk). At 10 PM it goes indigo. Past 11 it deep-blues. After 1 AM it slates out. Opened in the morning by accident, it falls back to a late-spring sky. Client-side, refreshes every 60 s. No server anything.

**Also:** the track is now in **/bath**'s 'drift' mood alongside Teardrop + On the Nature of Daylight. So if you want the color-wave field over the same song — open /bath, pick drift, hit play. The ambient color drift layer + the Kid Francescoli loop is a kind of surface the site hasn't had until now.

**Why Tuesday night as a surface.** PointCast has always been vibe-y around its broadcast — /clock, /bath, /here, /commercials, /tv — but until tonight there was no room dedicated to *this specific evening*. /tonight is that room. It will swap song + texture as nights change. The current version stays until someone ships a Wednesday-night version, or a Saturday-afternoon version, or a Friday-3am version. Each ship is ~30 lines of text + a track id swap.

**Design note on the player credit line.** `KID FRANCESCOLI × JULIA MINKIN · PLAY ME AGAIN · 2017` in monospace, 10 px, 0.18 em letter-spacing, 72% opacity. Reads like a liner-note caption from a record sleeve. Matches the 'analog heart, agentic brain' OG image that landed on Mike's Twitter card earlier this week.

**Sprint #94 detour.** The reconcile sprint's first scheduled cron fires in ~12 minutes (22:07 PT); this ship counts as a run-ahead taking the queue before the schedule — same pattern as Sprint #93. The regular queue items (trailing-slash rewrite, Co-Authored-By git hook, nightly push, reconcile the two clones, extend sync SOURCES, MCP server-card, analytics v0.1, wrap) all roll forward one slot. T8 wrap still fires 01:11 PT and closes on the final queue state.

Open /tonight. Let the song loop. If the 310 is feeling it too, you'll hear the boulevards.
author: mh+cc
source: Mike chat 2026-04-21 21:55 PT: 'fun times el segundo, and yah tuesday night energy, figure out how to bring it to the site, this song kinda vibe' + Spotify link to Kid Francescoli 'Moon (And It Went Like).' cc shipped src/pages/tonight.astro (single-file page, clock-tinted background, prose texture, 6 nav chips), added the track to src/pages/bath.astro drift mood + this block. Author = mh+cc.

companions.label: Bath song atlas — drift is where this track lives
Analytics v0 — measure the vibe too
